tiers.label: Adjacent (within 200 ft of station)
tiers.notes: Strongest allowance. Buildings over 85 ft trigger SB 423 prevailing-wage / skilled-and-trained labor standards.

tiers.label: Within 1/4 mile of a Tier 1 station
tiers.notes: Local height/density/FAR limits below these floors are preempted.

tiers.label: Within 1/2 mile of a Tier 1 station
tiers.notes: Outer ring. Still meaningfully more than most current Palo Alto downtown caps.

tier1.label: Tier 1
tier1.definition: Heavy rail or commuter rail with 72+ trains per day
examples: Caltrain (incl. Palo Alto, Cal Ave, San Antonio)

tier2.label: Tier 2
tier2.definition: Light rail, BRT, or commuter rail with 48-71 trains per day

stations.name: Palo Alto (University Ave) Caltrain
stations.context: Downtown Palo Alto. Surrounded by University Ave retail, parking lots, low-rise commercial.

stations.name: California Avenue Caltrain
stations.context: Restaurant row, mix of low-rise commercial and residential.

stations.name: San Antonio Caltrain
stations.context: Border of Palo Alto and Mountain View. Office and shopping.

paloAlto.cityResponse: Palo Alto staff are exploring rezoning and a TOD 'alternative plan' to limit SB 79's effects while still meeting equivalent zoned-capacity targets.

process.approval: Ministerial under SB 35 path — qualifying projects bypass CEQA and discretionary review.
process.preemption: Local zoning that conflicts with the SB 79 floors is preempted, unless the city adopts a qualifying TOD alternative plan.

sources.title: Palo Alto Daily Post — 7-story buildings near Caltrain
Palo Alto Online — city's rezoning response
Palo Alto Online — downtown / San Antonio impact
